commentary_text: The sportsbooks assign Norway a 79% win probability versus the model's 63%, a 16-percentage-point gap favoring the favorite. The market is also notably more skeptical of an Iraq victory, pricing it at 7% compared to the model's 12%. This divergence suggests the market may be responding to recent form, injury status, or lineup changes that have not yet entered the model's estimate; such information could genuinely sharpen conviction around Norway's advantage. However, the dashboard surfaces this gap as a signal of information asymmetry rather than proof that either source has correctly read the match.
